title: Gavilea leucantha
taxonomy: Gavilea leucantha is classified within the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Gavilea, which is a group of flowering plants native to New Zealand and Australia. The species Gavilea leucantha is further classified as a type of terrestrial orchid, with the specific epithet leucantha referring to its white flowers. More specifically, it is a member of the subfamily Orchidoideae and the tribe Gastrodieae. The classification of Gavilea leucantha reflects its evolutionary relationships and characteristics within the plant kingdom.

intro: Gavilea leucantha, also known as the white-flowered Gavilea, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its showy white flowers and ability to thrive in well-drained soils. The plant is reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the prevention of infections. The bioactive constituents of Gavilea leucantha include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Gavilea leucantha is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, as well as in traditional medicines, to leverage its potential health benefits.
